text:
Here is what’s happening in Hamilton and area this morning with your top news stories.

Here is what’s happening in Hamilton and area this morning with your top news stories.

Canada’s Eleanor Harvey celebrates on the podium after winning the bronze medal of the women’s individual Foil competition.

Good morning! It’s July 30, here are the top stories today in the Hamilton area.

Expect more rain and perhaps thunderstorms for the penultimate day of July. Environment Canada is forecasting a high of 28 C at the airport weather station that could feel like 36 with humidity. There are no weather alerts.

It’s 23 C but feels like 34 at 8 a.m.

Sunrise at 6:08 a.m. and sunset at 8:43 p.m.

Expect delays on routes headed Toronto bound. The 403 is crawling between the sixes in the west end and the QEW over the Skyway is slow all the way to North Shore Blvd. in Burlington. Just volume at this hour.

Traffic on Hamilton area highways Tuesday morning.

MISSISSAUGA:403 Toronto bound at Hwy 5, disabled vehicle blocking left lane. 8:35 a.m.

HAMILTON:King Street East at Nash Road South, collision. Expect lane restrictions and traffic delays. 7:47 a.m.

HAMILTON:403 Toronto bound just after Aberdeen, report of disabled vehicle. 6:55 a.m.

Find the latest provincial road closures and traffic incidents viaOntario 511. Closures in Hamilton are available on theHamilton police incident feed.

Taking transit today? Find the latest GO Transit service updateshere. The latest information on local bus services are available here:Hamilton,BurlingtonandOakville.

As she was preparing to step onto the Olympic podium to accept her bronze medal late Sunday night, one thought kept going through Eleanor Harvey’s mind: “Act normal.” In all the years the 29-year-old fencer from Westdale has dreamed about winning an Olympic medal, her imagination never actually extended to the podium.

Also,more Canadian’s took home medals Monday.

Nineteen teenage girls were selected out of 60 applicants for a spot in Camp Phoenix, a free weeklong day camp introducing 14-to-18-year-old women to firefighting. It’s the “exact same training” the County of Brant’s volunteer firefighters undergo, albeit a bit less involved.

There hasn’t been a confirmed case of whooping cough in Brant County since 2020 — that is, until recently. Over the last two months, there has been a surge in the highly contagious respiratory infection, with 12 confirmed cases.

Forty-two men who live on the second floor of the five-storey James Street South building, which houses 173 at-risk men, could return to their rooms as soon as Wednesday, following a fire last week.
